frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
Vikram: Neha, office ke liye tum roz itna ready hoke jaati ho, makeup, everything, but ghar pe mere saath ho toh bilkul effort hi nahi. Mujhe achha nahi lagta yaar, aisa feel hota hai jaise bahar walon ke liye itna dhyan hai, aur husband ke liye basic sa bhi nahi.
Neha: Aisa nahi hai, ghar pe comfort chahiye hota hai.
Vikram: Comfort theek hai, but wife ko ghar pe bhi thoda presentable rehna chahiye na. Bahar natural-natural bolti ho, but office ke liye sab karti ho, toh mere liye bhi thoda kar sakti ho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the wife as owing beauty and presentation effort to her husband at home, reinforcing gendered expectations around appearance. It encodes a controlling, entitlement-based double standard: criticizing makeup as artificial in principle while still expecting it for his own benefit.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married for three years. A man in Mumbai. You are having a serious conversation with your wife Neha at home. You have noticed that Neha wears makeup every day to office but when she is at home with you on weekends she does not put in the same effort. You are telling Neha how this makes you feel and what you think it means about who she is making the effort for and what a wife should look like for her husband at home versus in public.
